text: Fill in the blanks — Lungs and Trachea
questions: The trachea is *10–11/10-11* cm long and extends from *C6* to the level of T4/T5, where it bifurcates at the *carina*.
The right main bronchus is *wider/broader*, shorter, and more *vertical* than the left, making it the common site for foreign body aspiration.
The right lung has *3/three* lobes and *10/ten* bronchopulmonary segments, while the left lung has *2/two* lobes.
The trachea has 16–20 *C-shaped/hyaline* cartilage rings that are open *posteriorly*, bridged by the trachealis muscle.
Needle insertion for chest drainage should be over the *upper/superior* border of the lower rib to avoid the intercostal neurovascular bundle.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review the material and try again.
Good effort! Review the sections you missed.
